verdicts.one_line: Both are genuine eco-evolutionary game dynamics: payoff-matrix-driven frequency-dependent (replicator/mutation-selection) strategy dynamics coupled to an ecological population/resource variable with a single growth-rate/carrying-capacity control parameter tuning distinct regimes, with spite/fairness strategies mapping to prey types and resource growth to carrying capacity. Real but within the same evolutionary-dynamics family.

Reject: the only common thread is the generic maximum-entropy/Jaynes slogan that 'constraints fix the distribution' — Paper A physically thermalizes energy-and-norm-conserving coupled oscillators to a Rayleigh-Jeans equilibrium, while Paper B does epistemic entropic inference to derive a Merton jump-diffusion; same meta-principle, different machinery.

Both construct a simplicial complex from a discrete set of observations and read off its homology/Betti numbers as topological invariants (simplicial TDA): worldline measurement projections map to Takens-embedded S&P return points, and spacetime topology maps to financial-time-series topology. Specific load-bearing shared structure with near-zero field overlap.

Both cast the problem as a Fokker-Planck/diffusion PDE for the probability density of a stochastic variable evolving in a time-like parameter — magnification in Persson's contact theory, real time in option pricing — with contact pressure mapping to log-price and magnification to time; B adds a Kolmogorov-Feller jump term but the shared governing equation is the diffusion PDE.

Both solve the linearized Boltzmann transport equation (Liouville streaming operator plus collision integral) for the out-of-equilibrium perturbation of a distribution function to extract a transport/dissipation quantity — e-e collisions map to plasma interaction rates, B-field streaming to the wall-sourced source term — but the Boltzmann equation is a universal kinetic equation any physicist recognizes in both.

Both model a spatial point pattern as a Cox process whose intensity is driven by a latent Gaussian field, with a single scalar coupling (galaxy bias b / LGCP loading) quantifying how strongly a second co-located observable (CMB-lensing convergence / store survival) traces the same latent field, inferred via cross-correlation (P_gK / joint-likelihood loading). Precise role-for-role mapping across cosmology and spatial statistics.

summary.overall: Five of the six are genuine shared-mechanism bridges; E1 is the lone rejection. The most compelling by structural fidelity are E4 (literally the same linearized Boltzmann equation, though textbook-universal) and E5 (a latent-Gaussian-field-driven Cox process with a scalar cross-correlation loading, a precise and surprising cosmology-to-spatial-statistics mapping); E2 (simplicial homology from discrete observations) and E3 (Fokker-Planck with a time-like parameter, magnification as time) are also genuine surprising structural mappings, while E0 is real but sits within the shared evolutionary-game-dynamics family. E1 fails because it rests only on the generic maximum-entropy/Jaynes slogan, bridging a physical Rayleigh-Jeans thermalization to epistemic entropic inference — same meta-principle, different machinery.
